comment: Quality Gates catalog — code dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/code/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/code/.

sources: scripts/check_clippy_lint_loaded.sh (migrated to quality/gates/code/clippy-lint-loaded.sh per SIMPLIFY-04 P58 Wave C)

asserts: clippy.toml lists each of: reqwest::Client::new, reqwest::Client::builder, reqwest::ClientBuilder::new under [disallowed-methods]
no source file outside crates/reposix-core/src/http.rs constructs reqwest::Client / reqwest::ClientBuilder directly

rows.owner_hint: if RED: clippy.toml is missing or has been edited to drop a reqwest entry; OR a new file constructs reqwest::Client directly outside http.rs (security regression — see CLAUDE.md threat-model § 'Outbound HTTP allowlist')

asserts: the most recent ci.yml `test` job for the current branch exited 0

rows.owner_hint: if RED: ci.yml test job is failing for the current branch -- run gh run view <run-id> --log-failed. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr and demoted to on-demand -- ci.yml's dedicated `test` job (cargo test --workspace --locked) is the canonical enforcement venue; this row's own verifier only polls `gh run list` for the most recent successful run (needs gh CLI + auth, and is circular when run from inside the very CI run it's checking). Kept on-demand for manual post-hoc audits (e.g. "did main's last CI run actually pass").

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): reason still true -- cargo nextest run --workspace still consumes 6-15 minutes, still violates the CLAUDE.md ONE-cargo-at-a-time memory-budget rule, and still exceeds the pre-pr 10-minute cadence cap on this VM. ci.yml's test job remains the canonical enforcement venue. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P97 (Good-to-haves polish + milestone close, launch-readiness slot) -- per-row local cargo enforcement alternatives (or formally retiring this row in favor of CI-only)

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the most recent ci.yml test job for the branch actually failed, `gh run list` would report a non-success conclusion for that run and the verifier would exit non-zero naming it -- a real CI failure is the falsifying evidence, not a local proxy signal.

sources: scripts/check_fixtures.py (migrated to quality/gates/code/check-fixtures.py per SIMPLIFY-05 P58 Wave C, Option A)

asserts: github_issues.json: 4-12 KB JSON array, >=3 issues, required keys present, no credential-shaped strings
confluence_pages.json: 6-16 KB JSON object with results[], >=3 pages, ADF body re-parseable, no secrets/PII
fixtures/README.md: contains required terms (offline, content_hash, github_issues.json, confluence_pages.json, synthetic)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a benchmark fixture file has been edited (added secrets, dropped required keys, drifted size). Investigate via `python3 quality/gates/code/check-fixtures.py` locally; do NOT silence.

sources: .githooks/pre-push:61 (invokes python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence pre-push, which composes this row)

asserts: cargo fmt --all -- --check exits 0 (no formatting drift in any workspace crate)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: cargo fmt regressed; run cargo fmt --all locally and commit the result. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- ci.yml's dedicated `fmt` job runs the identical command with rust-cache reuse; a second cargo fmt in quality-pre-pr would be pure duplicate compute. Row still gates pre-push locally.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If any workspace crate had formatting drift, `cargo fmt --all -- --check` would exit non-zero and print the offending diff, which the verifier greps for and reports as FAIL -- the 'no formatting drift' claim is falsified directly by the tool this row wraps, not by a proxy signal.

sources: .githooks/pre-push:61 (invokes python3 quality/runners/run.py --cadence pre-push, which composes this row)
rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new clippy violation; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --fix to auto-fix where possible. D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr -- ci.yml's dedicated `clippy` job runs the identical command with rust-cache reuse; a second clippy pass in quality-pre-pr would double compile time for no new signal. Row still gates pre-push locally. Pivot rule: if cargo clippy in pre-push exceeds 60s on warm cache, P60 Wave E may move it OUTSIDE the runner; document in SURPRISES.md.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Substrate already exists (commit ca7cb61): capabilities_match_create_impl tests in reposix-github, reposix-jira, reposix-confluence.
rows.command: cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match

asserts: cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match exits 0
at least 3 tests matched and ran (guards against a silent 0-matches false-pass if a test name or --lib filter drifts)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a connector's CAPABILITIES constant claims support for an operation (e.g. BackendFeature::Create) that create_record actually returns Error::NotSupported for, or vice versa -- check the connector's supports() impl against its create_record match arms
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs `cargo test -p reposix-github -p reposix-jira -p reposix-confluence capabilities_match` and parses the libtest summary lines to confirm >=3 tests ran and none failed. If the claim ('each connector's CAPABILITIES constant agrees with observable create_record behavior') were false -- e.g. a connector's supports(Create) returned true while create_record actually returned Err(NotSupported), or the test filter matched 0 tests because a test was renamed/deleted -- the assertion would fail because cargo test would report a FAILED test or the parsed ran-count would be 0, and the verifier would exit non-zero naming which.

asserts: coverable-line counter validated within 15% of kcov on all executed scripts

rows.owner_hint: if RED: (a) 'kcov is available' fails -> install it: sudo apt-get install -y kcov (the gate/CI both depend on it; on a dev box without kcov the P2 row degrades to non-blocking locally, but the CI shell-coverage job hard-fails). (b) the aggregate dropped below the ratchet floor in quality/shell-coverage-floor.txt -> a shell script lost test coverage or a new never-run script entered the corpus; add a harness under quality/gates/code/shell-coverage-tests/ to exercise it, or (only if the drop is justified) lower the floor with a note. (c) the counter-validation assert fails -> coverable_line_count in scripts/shell_coverage.py drifted >15% from kcov on an executed script; retune it. NEVER raise the floor above the measured aggregate to force-pass.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs scripts/shell_coverage.py, which drives every harness under quality/gates/code/shell-coverage-tests/ through kcov, merges the runs, and computes the aggregate line-coverage across the full in-scope shell corpus (never-run scripts counted at 0% in the denominator). If the claim ('aggregate shell coverage is at or above the floor, and the coverable-line counter tracks kcov within 15%') were false -- coverage regressed below quality/shell-coverage-floor.txt, or the Python line-counter drifted from kcov's own total on an executed script -- the driver appends the failing assertion to asserts_failed and exits 1, and the gate propagates that non-zero exit; a passing floor and a clean counter are the falsifying evidence, not a proxy signal.
